nodes.name: United States
nodes.summary: A country in North America, where the Federal Reserve is located.

nodes.name: Federal Reserve
nodes.summary: The central bank of the United States, responsible for setting interest rates.

nodes.name: Jerome Powell
nodes.summary: The Chair of the Federal Reserve, who makes statements about interest rates and the economy.

nodes.name: Google
nodes.summary: A technology company that announced layoffs.

nodes.name: Meta
nodes.summary: A technology company that confirmed layoffs.

nodes.name: Twitter
nodes.summary: A social media company acquired by Elon Musk.

nodes.name: S&P 500
nodes.summary: A stock market index that tracks the performance of 500 large companies.

nodes.name: Russia
nodes.summary: A country that is disrupting gas supplies to Europe.

nodes.name: Europe
nodes.summary: A continent that is being affected by Russia's gas supply disruptions.

nodes.name: Consumer confidence
nodes.summary: A measure of how confident consumers are in the economy.

nodes.name: Venture capital
nodes.summary: An industry that invests in startups and early-stage companies.

nodes.name: Housing market
nodes.summary: An industry that is being affected by high mortgage rates.
